name: cds_hazard_calib_then_price_off_par
description: Synthetic single-name hazard calibrate-then-price integration fixture with flattened hazard and off-par CDS outputs.

provenance.source_detail: Deterministic synthetic credit integration fixture with flattened hazard calibration and CDS pricing outputs
provenance.regen_command: manual formula fixture; flattened outputs committed in fixture

source_validation.reason: Nested integration calibration contains final curve/parameter outputs but lacks executable quote/source instruments; this section is source-validation only while the pricing leg executes.
source_validation.required_for_execution: Add executable market quotes/source instruments and wire this integration fixture to the real calibration engine so calibration metrics are computed from residuals.

source_reference.priced_instrument: 5Y CDS using the surviving Bloomberg CDSW pricing fixture

source_validation.reason: Integration fixture contains source hazard/pricing outputs, but the nested calibration leg is source-validation and the nested CDS pricing leg still depends on source curve/risk reconciliation. Keep this non-gating until both legs execute like-for-like.
source_validation.required_for_execution: Reconcile the canonical pricing/calibration inputs and risk methodology to the external source, then remove this source_validation block so expected_outputs are compared by Rust and Python runners.

cds_npv.tolerance_reason: Matches pricing/cds/cds_5y_par_spread.json widened Bloomberg CDSW principal tolerance while preserving expected_outputs. abs-or-rel: absolute/display tolerance is the intended comparison gate; relative tolerance is retained only as a legacy diagnostic.

cds_cs01.tolerance_reason: Matches pricing/cds/cds_5y_par_spread.json widened Bloomberg CDSW spread DV01 tolerance while preserving expected_outputs. abs-or-rel: absolute/display tolerance is the intended comparison gate; relative tolerance is retained only as a legacy diagnostic.
